results.title: VideoMDM — 3D Human Motion Generation from 2D Supervision
results.content:
We introduce VideoMDM, a diffusion-based framework that trains 3D human motion priors directly from accurate 2D poses extracted from monocular videos, without any 3D ground truth. A pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter provides approximate 3D pose sequences that serve as a noisy teacher: these are diffused, denoised by the model in 3D, and supervised in 2D by reprojecting the prediction and comparing against accurate keypoints. We show that, under mild assumptions, a depth-weighted 2D reprojection loss is equivalent in expectation to direct 3D supervision, and we adapt standard 3D motion regularizers — velocity consistency and over-parameterized representation alignment — to this 2D setting. Unlike methods that lift 2D to 3D only at inference, VideoMDM learns a coherent 3D motion manifold during [...] VideoMDM learns a coherent 3D motion manifold during training. On HumanML3D it nearly closes the gap to fully 3D-supervised MDM (FID 0.88 vs 0.54); on real video datasets Fit3D and NBA the method learns to generate motions consistently preferred by humans, with strong quantitative results. [...] VideoMDM training pipeline 

VideoMDM training. From monocular video, we extract accurate 2D keypoints and approximate 3D poses. A motion diffusion model is trained to denoise the 3D poses under multi-source supervision: (i) 3D representation alignment, and (ii) 2D reprojection and velocity consistency with the accurate 2D pose.

   Motion Representation Alignment illustration

results.content:
We introduce VideoMDM, a diffusion-based framework that trains 3D human motion priors directly from accurate 2D poses extracted from monocular videos, without any 3D ground truth. A pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter provides approximate 3D pose sequences that serve as a noisy teacher: these are diffused, denoised by the model in 3D, and supervised in 2D by reprojecting the prediction and comparing against accurate keypoints. We show that, under mild assumptions, a depth-weighted 2D reprojection loss is equivalent in expectation to direct 3D supervision, and we adapt standard 3D motion regularizers - velocity consistency and over-parameterized representation alignment - to this 2D setting. Unlike methods that lift 2D to 3D only at inference, VideoMDM learns a coherent 3D motion manifold during [...] VideoMDM learns a coherent 3D motion manifold during training. On HumanML3D it nearly closes the gap to fully 3D-supervised MDM (FID 0.88 vs 0.54); On real video datasets Fit3D and NBA the method learns to generate motions consistently preferred by humans, with strong quantitative results. [...] Hugging Face's logo

# VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision

## Abstract

VideoMDM trains 3D human motion priors from 2D poses using a diffusion framework with 2D reprojection loss and 3D motion regularizers, achieving near-3D supervised performance without requiring 3D ground truth.

results.title: VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision | alphaXiv
results.content:
John: Precisely. State-of-the-art models like MDM produce excellent results but are fundamentally bottlenecked by their reliance on high-quality MoCap datasets. These datasets are captured in controlled studio environments and don't reflect the diversity of human movement we see in the real world. VideoMDM's primary objective is to break this dependency. It aims to train a 3D text-to-motion diffusion model directly from monocular videos, using only accurate 2D pose sequences as supervision. The goal is to learn a coherent 3D motion manifold without ever seeing ground-truth 3D data during training. This could unlock a massive new source of training data. [...] John: It's in the same spirit, yes, but the approach is different. VideoMDM's focus is on training a 3D-native generative model that learns the entire motion manifold. The significance is that it opens the door to training on a scale that was previously impossible. Imagine training a motion model on terabytes of YouTube videos. You could capture an unprecedented diversity of actions, from professional sports to everyday interactions, that you'd never find in a MoCap dataset. This could lead to models that generate far more realistic and varied motions for applications in animation, gaming, and robotics. It also provides a formal framework for this type of cross-modality learning, which could be applied to other 3D generation tasks. [...] Transcript

John: Welcome to our seminar on Advanced Topics in Computer Vision. Today's lecture is on 'VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision' by researchers from Technion and NVIDIA. We've seen a lot of recent work, like 'Geometry Forcing' and 'VideoGPA', focused on injecting 3D consistency into video diffusion models. This paper, however, tackles a related but distinct problem: how to train a native 3D motion generator using only 2D video supervision, bypassing the need for expensive 3D motion capture data. It represents a significant push towards data scalability in motion synthesis.

John: Yes, Noah?

results.title: VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision
results.content:
motions to 3D only at inference time [19, 25], which prevents the model from learning a consistent

3D prior, or that depend on 3D supervision for fine-tuning , VideoMDM trains a diffusion model

natively in 3D space using only 2D supervision. Our formulation opens, for the first time, a path

towards large-scale training of 3D text-to-motion diffusion from monocular videos without any

MoCap data.

Building on cross-modality training of Image-to-3D diffusion , we adopt a noisy-teacher scheme:

a pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter produces approximate 3D pose sequences from 2D inputs; these are

diffused, denoised by the model in 3D, and supervised in 2D by reprojecting the prediction and

comparing against accurate keypoints from video. This design lets the model learn a coherent 3D [...] identities, and viewpoints. Harnessing such in-the-wild data could enable scalable and diverse 3D

motion generation. However, most videos are monocular, lacking the multi-view cues necessary for

reliable 3D reconstruction. While monocular 3D pose and motion estimators [57, 41, 48, 25] remain

noisy and ambiguous, 2D keypoint detectors [5, 8, 18] have reached high accuracy and robustness.

The key challenge, therefore, is how to train a generative 3D motion model using only accurate 2D

supervision derived from monocular video.

We address this challenge by introducing VideoMDM, a diffusion-based framework for training 3D

human motion models entirely from 2D pose supervision. Unlike prior approaches that triangulate 2D [...] ground truth is available for them (Section 3.5).

We evaluate VideoMDM in three regimes. (i) On a 2D-only version of HumanML3D , where 2D

poses are obtained by projecting MoCap, VideoMDM achieves FID 0.88 — nearly closing the gap to

fully 3D-supervised MDM (FID 0.54) and improving on the strongest 2D-supervised baseline by

roughly x2. (ii) On Fit3D  — a real-world setting, where training uses monocular fitness video

with extracted 2D keypoints and no 3D supervision — VideoMDM halves joint error against WHAM

 on motions far outside the lifter’s distribution (MPJPE 111 vs 228 mm) and produces 5.5×

smoother motion (Accel 3.16 vs 17.66 m/s²). And is preferred by humans in generation against all

baselines. (iii) On the NBA dataset , VideoMDM is preferred over MAS in 64% of pairwise

# VideoMDM

⚠️ This is a temporary README — a complete one will follow.

Official code for "VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision"
(project page · paper).

VideoMDM trains 3D human-motion diffusion models (text-to-motion) using only 2D pose
supervision extracted from monocular video — no 3D ground truth. It is built on top of
MDM (Human Motion Diffusion Model).

## Usage

This repository follows the structure and conventions of the upstream MDM repository by
Guy Tevet. For environment setup, dependency/asset downloads, and the general
training / sampling / evaluation workflow, please follow the upstream instructions there:

results.title: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision - arXiv
results.content:
## 5 Conclusion, Limitations and Future Work

We have presented VideoMDM, a training method for 3D human motion diffusion using only 2D supervision, and demonstrated its ability to generate high-quality motions that in some settings nearly match the performance of fully 3D-supervised methods. Our cross-modality diffusion owes its success to a set of stabilization techniques and natural motion regularizations formulated in 2D. VideoMDM makes a significant stride towards training motion diffusion models directly from abundant monocular videos. Such capability opens up new possibilities for learning generative priors of real-world motion distributions such as multi-person behaviors and human-object interactions which are otherwise difficult to acquire.

#### Limitations. [...] VideoMDM achieves its strongest results with ground-truth camera parameters, which are unavailable for most in-the-wild videos. PnP-estimated cameras recover most of the gap on synthetic HumanML3D but leave a larger drop on Fit3D, where camera estimation noise compounds with lifter and pose-extraction noise; better camera estimators would directly translate into better priors. Our method also depends on a pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter as a noisy teacher: although the learned prior generalizes substantially beyond the lifter’s distribution (Section˜4.2), domains where no reasonable lifter is available (e.g. non-human motion) remain out of reach. All settings we evaluate contain no or only minimal occlusions; extending to occluded settings such as those in  is necessary for fully in-the-wild [...] Our central claim is that 2D supervision from monocular video unlocks 3D motion distributions inaccessible to MoCap-bound training. We test this on Fit3D : real videos using 2D-pose extraction, and motions far outside the distribution captured by the lifters. Fit3D contains 611 training sequences across 37 fitness exercises, with synchronized video and accurate 3D ground truth used only for evaluation. Many of these motions have no analog in HumanML3D (e.g. mule kicks, burpees, stretches). For each sequence we randomly select one camera, extract 2D poses with RTMPose . Approximate 3D poses are obtained from the same video using WHAM , a dedicated video-to-3D method, serving as the noisy teacher. Camera positions are provided in the dataset or estimated using PnP. We initialize VideoMDM

results.title: What is Video Management Software (VMS)? Features, Types, and How to Choose
results.content:
# What is Video Management Software (VMS)? Features, Types, and How to Choose

Video management software (VMS) is the central control platform for recording, storing, retrieving, and analyzing video from security cameras. A VMS functions as the hub of monitoring systems, managing multiple cameras simultaneously, storing footage locally or in the cloud, providing live viewing and playback capabilities, and applying artificial intelligence analytics to video content. Modern VMS solutions are essential infrastructure that determines whether security investments deliver actionable intelligence or simply record footage nobody accesses.

## What is video management software (VMS)? [...] Motion detection and smart alerts: Detect movement in defined zones with distinction between motion types (person, vehicle, animal) and false positives (wind, rain). Advanced smart alerts include object detection, behavioral detection (loitering, running, falling), intrusion detection, customizable response levels, and automatic actions (higher frame rates, personnel notification, access control activation).

User permissions and audit trails: Enforce granular permissions restricting user viewing, recording, deletion capabilities based on roles. Document every action (who viewed what when, configuration changes, video exports) with tamper-proof audit logs critical for regulatory compliance (HIPAA, GDPR, SOX) and incident investigation. [...] ### What happens if the internet goes down?

WiFi-enabled cloud-only systems may lose remote access but cameras continue recording locally. Footage typically syncs when the internet is restored. Hybrid systems help maintain full local operation. On-premise systems operate completely independently, ideal for unreliable connectivity.

### How do I know if my VMS is secure?

Verify authentication (strong passwords, multi-factor), network encryption (HTTPS/SSL), data encryption (at-rest and in-transit), audit trails (all access logged), regular patching, and third-party certifications. Red flags: no audit logging, weak defaults, delayed security updates.

results.title: What Is a Video Management System (VMS)? - Hanwha Vision
results.content:
## What is VMS (Video Management System)?

A Video Management System (VMS) is software that connects to cameras and other video devices to manage video footage. Unlike older, hardware-based systems like DVRs (Digital Video Recorders) and NVRs (Network Video Recorders), a VMS is built to scale across vast networks with hundreds or thousands of cameras across multiple sites.

Supporting both cloud and hybrid deployments, it acts as a central hub for uses like live monitoring, recording, playback, device configuration, and analytics.

In modern enterprise environments, it also functions as the command layer of a broader security ecosystem, integrating with access control systems, alarms, and IT infrastructure to support real-time decision-making and response. [...] ### About Hanwha Vision America

Hanwha Vision America is redefining what’s possible in safety, operations, and business intelligence, going beyond security with its continually expanding ecosystem of surveillance & vision solutions. Built on a foundation of trust, innovation in optical design, image processing, and cybersecurity, Hanwha Vision technologies combine 24/7 protection, Artificial Intelligence and cloud-based platforms with the data-powered analytics customers need to generate actionable market insights.

Hanwha Vision America is committed to developing intelligent solutions that keep businesses moving forward, empowering organizations to see more, understand deeper, and respond smarter. Learn more about Hanwha Vision. [...] ### Intelligent Analytics

Modern systems apply AI and machine learning to automatically analyze video streams and surface meaningful insights.

Capabilities extend beyond basic motion detection to include object detection, facial recognition (where permitted), license plate recognition, and behavioral analytics like loitering or intrusion detection.

Instead of monitoring dozens of screens, operators now receive alerts when specific conditions are met — such as a person entering a restricted zone after hours or an unattended object appearing in a public space. This not only improves efficiency but also allows teams to focus on high-risk situations rather than routine monitoring.

### System Integration

results.title: What Is a Video Management System (VMS)? Benefits & Top Solutions
results.content:
Built on proprietary AI, the system goes beyond simple object recognition to interpret behaviors and detect suspicious activity with near-human-like awareness. It can also scan millions of hours of footage in seconds, filter results by precise attributes, and simplify sharing with secure links, email, or SMS.

Beyond security, the platform transforms raw video into actionable intelligence. Customizable dashboards uncover patterns in foot traffic, production workflows, safety compliance, and more—empowering smarter decisions across operations.

With NDAA-compliant hardware, industry-leading AI, and a flexible hybrid-cloud architecture, the solution is designed for sectors including education, government, manufacturing, retail, healthcare, and hospitality. [...] ## How do video management systems work?

A VMS works by coordinating the full lifecycle of video—ingest, processing, storage, access, and correlation—so security teams can monitor live activity, investigate incidents quickly, and maintain policy-driven retention. It links cameras, storage, analytics, and user interfaces into one environment, replacing fragmented tools with a single operational view. At a high level, the process is as follows: [...] Resources

Lumana / Blog / Security management / What Is a Video Management System (VMS)? Benefits & Solutions

# What Is a Video Management System (VMS)? Benefits & Solutions

September 23, 2025

Reading time: 7 min

Subscribe to Lumana Insights on Linkedin

Sign up

Security teams are expected to see everything, verify rapidly, and respond without delay. Yet siloed cameras, manual searches, and alert fatigue slow them down. A video management system (VMS) addresses this by unifying video across sites, aligning monitoring and alerts, streamlining search, and enabling operators to respond quickly.
